attacking_style: Shows aggressive intent on final climbs (8km to go in Lombardia, late-stage attacks in Vuelta) but struggles to sustain efforts beyond 20km ascents or multi-climb stages. Capable of solo breakaway wins when given freedom (Morzine victory).
team_tactics: Often receives domestique support when struggling (PIDCOCK rescue, CARAPAZ pacing, INEOS teammates after crashes), but can be left isolated when following moves without support (Strade Bianche). Team priority ambiguous—sometimes protected, sometimes in support role despite strong climbing form.
terrain_preference: Single decisive mountain stages with aggressive descents suit him best (Morzine stage win, top-5 Lombardia). Exposed on queen stages with multiple summit finishes and extended 20km+ climbs where he repeatedly faded.
vulnerability: Crashes frequently on technical sections (Tour descent crash, Catalunya flat roads, Vuelta crash affecting performance). Stage race accumulation causes visible deterioration in final week—dropped positions late in both 2022 Vuelta (7th after better early placement) and caught by rivals in Tour week 3.
key_patterns: Crashes on technical terrain repeatedly requiring teammate rescue or compromising subsequent climbing performance (4 incidents)
Fades dramatically in final week of Grand Tours, losing GC positions after strong first two weeks (3 observations)
Aggressive descending ability when healthy, holds off chasers and attacks in monuments' final kilometers (2 observations)
Struggles on extended 20km+ ascents and multi-climb queen stages, repeatedly dropped from GC group (3 observations)
Shows breakthrough climbing form early in stage races but cannot sustain through three weeks (Vuelta 4th to 7th decline)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour showed same pattern as 2022 Vuelta—strong mid-race performance (stage win) followed by third-week collapse (caught by Pogačar, crashed under pressure). Chronic inability to handle three-week accumulation despite improving one-day/single-stage results.
